exercises.title: FULL ROSTER
exercises.brief: Pull the whole file, in order.
exercises.prompt: The **bounties(id, name, reward, planet)** table is your active case file. Return **all columns** for **every** bounty, ordered by **id** (lowest first).
exercises.setup: CREATE TABLE bounties (id INTEGER, name TEXT, reward INTEGER, planet TEXT);INSERT INTO bounties VALUES (1,'Asimov',2500000,'Mars'),(2,'Teddy',1000000,'Mars'),(3,'Hex',800000,'Ganymede'),(4,'Pierrot',7000000,'Earth');
exercises.solution: SELECT * FROM bounties ORDER BY id;
exercises.hint: Use SELECT * for all columns, then ORDER BY id.
exercises.lore: Every face in the file gets a number before it gets a price.

exercises.title: KNOWN HIDEOUTS
exercises.prompt: From **bounties(id, name, reward, planet)**, return the list of **distinct planet** values — each planet exactly once. No specific order is required.
exercises.setup: CREATE TABLE bounties (id INTEGER, name TEXT, reward INTEGER, planet TEXT);INSERT INTO bounties VALUES (1,'Asimov',2500000,'Mars'),(2,'Teddy',1000000,'Mars'),(3,'Hex',800000,'Ganymede'),(4,'Pierrot',7000000,'Earth');
exercises.hint: SELECT DISTINCT planet collapses repeats into one row each.
exercises.lore: Mars shows up twice in the logs, but it's still one red rock.

exercises.title: OFF MARS
exercises.prompt: From **bounties(id, name, reward, planet)**, return the **name** and **planet** of every bounty whose **planet is not 'Mars'**, sorted by **name** alphabetically (A->Z).
exercises.setup: CREATE TABLE bounties (id INTEGER, name TEXT, reward INTEGER, planet TEXT);INSERT INTO bounties VALUES (1,'Asimov',2500000,'Mars'),(2,'Teddy',1000000,'Mars'),(3,'Hex',800000,'Ganymede'),(4,'Pierrot',7000000,'Earth');
exercises.starter: SELECT name, planet FROM bounties WHERE planet = 'Mars';
exercises.solution: SELECT name, planet FROM bounties WHERE planet <> 'Mars' ORDER BY name;
exercises.hint: Use WHERE planet <> 'Mars' (or !=), then ORDER BY name.
exercises.lore: The smart ones never linger on the same rock as the law.

exercises.title: MID-TIER MARKS
exercises.prompt: From **bounties(id, name, reward, planet)**, return **name** and **reward** for every bounty whose **reward is between 1,000,000 and 3,000,000 inclusive**, ordered by **reward** ascending (lowest first).
exercises.setup: CREATE TABLE bounties (id INTEGER, name TEXT, reward INTEGER, planet TEXT);INSERT INTO bounties VALUES (1,'Asimov',2500000,'Mars'),(2,'Teddy',1000000,'Mars'),(3,'Hex',800000,'Ganymede'),(4,'Pierrot',7000000,'Earth');
exercises.starter: SELECT name, reward FROM bounties ORDER BY reward;
exercises.solution: SELECT name, reward FROM bounties WHERE reward BETWEEN 1000000 AND 3000000 ORDER BY reward;
exercises.hint: WHERE reward BETWEEN 1000000 AND 3000000 keeps the band, then ORDER BY reward.
exercises.lore: Not chump change, not a fortune. Just enough to refuel.

exercises.title: CHEAP ON MARS
exercises.brief: Low-reward marks on the red planet.
exercises.prompt: From **bounties(id, name, reward, planet)**, return the **name** of every bounty that is **on Mars AND worth less than 2,000,000**, sorted by **name** alphabetically (A->Z).
exercises.setup: CREATE TABLE bounties (id INTEGER, name TEXT, reward INTEGER, planet TEXT);INSERT INTO bounties VALUES (1,'Asimov',2500000,'Mars'),(2,'Teddy',1000000,'Mars'),(3,'Hex',800000,'Ganymede'),(4,'Pierrot',7000000,'Earth');
exercises.starter: SELECT name FROM bounties WHERE planet = 'Mars';
exercises.solution: SELECT name FROM bounties WHERE planet = 'Mars' AND reward < 2000000 ORDER BY name;
exercises.hint: Chain two conditions with AND: planet = 'Mars' AND reward < 2000000.
exercises.lore: Barely worth the fuel, but Mars dust is cheap to walk through.
